name: 1. Night City Gangs & Corps - Associated Homebrew

text.content:
This module makes use of homebrew in mook abilities, alongside some minor, common-sense tweaks to weapons and gear that have already been integrated into the included sheets. Most of this is non-intrusive, and you should be able to make things work without it, if you prefer the defaults or your own way of doing things.

Dual Wielding

When wielding two melee weapons of the same type, add 1 to the RoF of one of them.

Otherwise, when wielding two different weapons, you may fire the full RoF of both, taking a cumulative -2 penalty to hit for each shot fired beyond what one weapon’s RoF provides.

Requires a BODY and DEX of 7 for Player Characters.

To remove, reduce RoF for melee weapons of most mooks by 1. No changes needed for ranged profiles.

More Cover Options

Cover has three stages: none, partial, and full, in that order. Moving from one stage to the next requires ½ an actor’s MOVE characteristic. You cannot skip a stage in this process without going prone. While in partial cover, attacks against the actor receive -4 to hit from ranged attacks, and the actor reduces rolls they make to fire out of cover by 4.

No action is required on your part to remove, though some stat blocks will make less sense under traditional rules, particularly those concerning shields.

Armor Ablates Easier

Any time a hit is scored against an opponent, even if that attack doesn’t pierce the armor, the armor still ablates by 1. Ignore this effect if the armor's remaining SP is 13 or higher.

Necessary to make medium and light weapon profiles remotely usable.

If you aren't using this rule, remove any mook armor with SP of 12 or higher and replace it with the default.

Cyber-Augmented Melee

If a linear frame is involved in using a melee weapon, or if your BODY score is 11 or higher, add 1d6 to your melee attacks. This damage is capped at 4d6 for Medium and Heavy Weapons, 5d6 for Very Heavy Weapons, and +1d6 to any melee weapons which innately due 5d6 damage or more.

Accounts for weirdo default rules where full-borg fighters are better off not using weapons.

To remove, reduce the melee damage of some mook profiles by 1-2d6.

On Luck

For any check which has been failed, players can retroactively choose to spend LUCK points to improve their result, at a 2:1 ratio.

Additionally, sometimes players might have a key check which they don't want to hard-fail. Before rolling a check, players may choose to spend 2 luck to negate a critical fail instead of increasing the result - if a critical fail is rolled, the result of 1 is taken, but a d10 isn't subtracted from the result.

Aggressive Autofire

Autofire DVs have been modestly reduced for SMGs and Assault Rifles, via the rollable tables included in the pack.

To implement at your table:

  1. Extract/import the included Aggressive Autofire DVs from the rollable table compendium.

  2. Create a new/empty rollable table compendium

  3. Re-save the tables you imported inside of it (the system won't recognize compendiums that are part of a module, for some reason, hence why you're doing this)

    1. Note: Do not include a folder when placing the DV tables into your new compendium.

  4. Under the Game Settings tab, go to Configure Settings -> Configure Compendia -> select your compendium's name for the DV tables.

Additionally, if an autofire attack hits with multiple bullets, if special ammunition is loaded, its effects are applied twice.

Exact changes:
SMGs: From 20/17/20/25/30 to 17/15/17/25/30

ARs: From 22/20/17/20/25 to 20/17/17/20/25

Weapon Equity

When rolling damage for a Very Heavy weapon, both 5s and 6s count for purposes of checking for critical injury. This bonus is also extended to sniper rifles.

When firing weapons that use Medium Pistol ammo or using a Light Melee Weapon, you may add +2 to your attack roll when attacking a target within 10m.

When dual wielding Medium Pistols, the first two shots fired from such a pistol aren’t considered when calculating cumulative negative penalties.

No in-line adjustments are necessary to remove these rules, though you may find that overall enemy lethality suffers if they are removed.

On Heavy Armor Penalties, and Heavy Armor On Enemies

Enemies equipped with heavy armor are equipped with an improved version intended as a direct substitute (see the item compendium for details), and the armor is already taken into consideration when their skills were created/are rolled. Most have had the penalties removed. Note that players equipped with these improved variants are unable to dodge bullets, regardless of their REF after equipping it.

On Status Effects - Poison, Sleep, Teargas, and Fire

The following changes apply:

  • How To Roll for Mooks: Most mooks should roll their physical skill against most status effects for Resist Drugs/Torture checks. Roll your chosen stat with a -2 penalty to reflect general stats vs a very particular set of effects.

  • Sleep, Poison and Biotoxin arrows apply their effects immediately, and reapply their effects once every round of combat (either on the attacker's turn, if alive, or at the end of the round, if they're removed from the scene) or every 30 seconds outside combat.

    • Sleep lasts for 10 triggers/10 rounds/5 minutes/until an antidote is administered.

      • Once the target is asleep, they no longer attempt the checks and remain unconscious for 1 minute in combat, 1 hour out of combat, until non-status-effect damage is taken, or until they're woken up as an action.

      • Once woken, if enough time hasn't passed, they may be at risk of falling back asleep!

    • Poison lasts for 10 triggers/10 rounds/5 minutes/until an antidote is administered.

    • Biotoxin lasts for 15 triggers/15 rounds/7.5 minutes/until an antidote is administered.

    • New applications of existing effects refresh the timer, but do not stack.

    • Biotoxin and poison effects do stack with one another, however, and do not cause a sleeping target to wake.

  • Poison, Biotoxin, Teargas and Sleep grenades linger for 1 minute in open areas, and for 5 minutes in enclosed spaces. In open spaces, the AOE might expand slightly during subsequent rounds at GM discretion.

    • At the start of the attacker's subsequent turns, the effects of these grenades reapply to anyone still standing in their AOE. Subsequent resistance checks are made with a cumulative -2 penalty (max -6)

  • Incendiary Grenades, Flamethrower AOEs and Molotov Cocktails linger for 1 minute after appearance.

    • Anyone hit by the initial explosion of an Incendiary Grenade is set Deadly On Fire (-6 HP/turn)

    • Anyone hit by the initial explosion of a Molotov Cocktail is set Strong On Fire (-4 HP/turn)

    • Anyone who moves through an active fire AOE that didn't start their turn there is set Mildly On Fire (-2 HP/turn)

    • Anyone already on fire (excluding a new status from bullet 3) that ends their turn in an active fire AOE has their status elevated by 1 stage.

    • Anyone on fire will burn until put out.

Krav Maga

Krav Maga was developed by Israeli Special Forces in conjunction with the US government - the project's goal was to produce a brutal, efficient, quick-learned martial art that was effective in disabling opponents quickly in open combat.

Technique: Groin Strike

No Requirement.

Instead of making 2 Attacks with your Martial Arts attack action, you can choose to make a Groin Strike. Use your action to make an aimed attack against the opponent's body, except that aimed attack is made with a -4 penalty, rather than a -8. If successful and the opponent does not have Pain Editor cyberware, that opponent must make a DV17 Will/Endurance check; on failure, they drop any held items and become suppressed. If they fail the check by 5 or more, they also fall prone. Damage dealt with this strike is inflicted as normal; if the damage reduces the target to 0 HP, the target is unconscious, but stable.

Technique: Erratic Aggression

Requirement: You dodged all Melee Attacks that were targeted at you during an opponent's turn (minimum 1), and have not used this ability since the start of the round.

Once per round when you fulfill this requirement after an opponent is finished attacking, you may use your Martial Arts Special Resolution to make one attack against an opponent which has just attacked you; the DV for that attack is the result of the enemy's last melee attack roll made against you.

Bojutsu

Bojutsu is a conjunction of martial arts of several different styles, primarily Karate, except specialized towards the use of a polearm/bowstaff. It allows fighters to add extra punch to their attacks without need for excessive muscle, while also remaining at range.

General Requirement: Bojutsu techniques can only be utilized while wielding a polearm/bowstaff/etc. in 2 hands. These weapons are considered to be Medium Melee weapons, and are typically around 6 feet long.

When wielding such a weapon with at least 1 point in Bojutsu, you may make attacks from up to 2 meters/yards (one space) away, and may add 1 to the weapons ROF.

You may use the martial arts skill when attacking with such weapons. Note: this assumes use of Cyber-Augmented Melee to add damage to attacks.

Technique: Polearm Master

Requirement: You did not hold an action during your last turn, and have not yet used this ability, this round.

On an enemy's turn, when an enemy comes within 2 m/yds of your position, you may make a martial arts attack against them which they dodge as normal.

Technique: Sentinel

Requirement: You hit a target with your first attack this turn, will not be attacking further this turn, and have not yet used this ability, this round.

Use the Martial Arts Special Move Resolution to attempt to beat a DV15. Upon success, the move characteristic of an opponent you attacked is reduced by 5 (minimum 1) until the start of your next turn. If you succeeded the check by 5 or more, the enemy also falls prone.

The Mook Pack includes several purchase-able automata, available in the item compendium. There are three methods of controlling drones, as follows:

1. Purchase A Demon

The default option, and how mook drones are controlled by default. Note: demon not included with purchase.

  • Once installed, the demon acts autonomously (under GM control), but is programmed to follow the user's commands to the best of its ability. It will not take orders from any other users unless directed to by the owner, and the other user remains recognizable as they were initially identified by the owner.

    • Always acts directly after the user's turn in initiative unless directed otherwise. Commanding it to take an action can be done once per turn as a free action, after which the drone will always attempt to take the first action in its queue.

    • The Demon's combat number (found above - almost always a 14) is used for rolling attack checks. Other checks are rolled with the Demon's Physical or Technical skill, specified on the drone.

      • Demon stats & prices can be found in the core rules on pg. 372.

      • Installed via control interface - if a demon is active in a drone, it cannot be controlled via any other method.

      • Once installed, a demon is destroyed if removed.

      • Optionally, the Demon can be ordered to relay footage from its drone camera to a particular device or network for monitoring.

2. Control Via Electronic Device

The cheap & simple option - almost always accessed via use of an @UUID[Compendium.cyberpunk-red-core.core_gear.GqYz689ddSKQHMen]{Agent} or @UUID[Compendium.cyberpunk-red-core.core_gear.1K1dtvrm7rjoraAJ]{Computer}.

  • The user of the device which is synced to the drone is able to control the drone as an action, directing it to do anything the drone would reasonably be able to do. The drone only moves or acts on the user's turn, and uses the user's skills to attempt checks which it would be able to attempt.

    • General control checks are made using the Cybertech skill. A minimum of 2 points in the skill is required to control a drone competently using this method.

    • Once synced to a computer, the drone will remain connected to the device until a demon is installed, it’s interfaced with, or the user deliberately disconnects the device.

    • The footage from the drone's camera can always be directly relayed to the user's device.

3. Control Via Interface Skill

The specialist's option - enabled via use of a Cyberdeck and the Interface skill.

  • The user uses a NET action to connect to the device any time it's within 10 m/yds of the user. If the drone is already connected to another network or controlled by a demon, a contested check may be necessary (at GM discretion - see Netrunning: Rebooted 2.0 for suggested DVs).

  • Once connected, the user controls the drone directly as a free action on each of their turns. It moves directly before or after them in initiative at the user's discretion, and can take one action that it would reasonably be able to attempt using the user's associated skill, potentially at a -4 if the user is also attempting a different action.

    • General control checks for the drone are made using the Interface skill. Controlling a drone this way requires a minimum of 2 points in the Interface skill to do competently.

    • If the user has a cyberdeck over their eyes, chyrons, etc., footage of the drone's cameras can be relayed to their HUD, enabling the user to see what the drone sees.
